qas.q: There are three lists that drivers, shippers, and carriers use to find out if a material is a regulated product. Which of the following is one of them?
qas.a: List of Hazardous Substances and Reportable Quantities

qas.q: A vehicle placarded for hazardous materials is required to have placards on how many sides of the vehicle?
A hazard class name or id number may not be used to describe a:
qas.a: Non Hazardous material

qas.q: The intention of the hazardous materials regulations are to ensure safe drivers and equipment, to communicate the risk, and
qas.a: To contain the material

qas.q: If and "x" or an "RQ" is written or typed into the HM column of a shipping paper entry, the:
qas.a: Shipment is regulated by the hazardous materials regulations

qas.q: Who do the regulations say is responsible for packaging, labeling and preparing the hazardous material shipping papers for shipment
qas.a: The shipper

qas.q: The power unit of a placarded vehicle must have a fire extinguisher with a UL rating of _____ B:C or more.
You may not park a vehicle carrying hazardous materials within how many feet of an open fire?
qas.a: 300 ft

qas.q: If hazardous material is leaking from your vehicle, you must not move your vehicle:
qas.a: Any more than safety requires

qas.q: The transport index of a radioactive material tells you
qas.a: The degree of control needed during transportation

qas.q: Which one of these shipping paper descriptions for a hazardous material is in the correct order?
qas.a: UN 1787, hydriodic acid, corrosive material, group

qas.q: A "W" in the first column of the hazardous materials table indicates:
qas.a: The entry applies to water shipments that are not an "RQ"

qas.q: You may not smoke around any vehicle being loaded or unloaded with:
qas.a:
A. Flammables
B. Oxidizers
C. Explosives
D. All of the above

qas.q: If a vehicle carrying explosives crashes with another object, you must not separate the vehicle from the object until:
qas.a: Someone has removed the explosives and placed them at least 200 feet away

qas.q: Before loading or unloading any explosive, you must check the cargo space for:
qas.a:
A. A cargo heater that could start
B. Sharp points that might damage the cargo
C. Loose floor boards or plates
D. All of the above

qas.q: A liquid poison is spilling from your vehicle. If it can be done safely, how should you channel the liquid?
qas.a: Away from streams or sewers

qas.q: Which emergency equipment may be used to warn of a stopped vehicle that contains explosives?
qas.a: Reflective triangles

qas.q: The description of a hazardous material must include the hazard class, the identification number and a proper shipping name. Which is required to be listed first on the shipping papers?
qas.a: The identification number

qas.q: When shippers package hazardous materials, they must certify that this was done according to the regulations. The only exception is when:
qas.a: The shipper is a private carrier carrying their own freight

qas.q: When you're transporting chlorine in cargo tanks, you must have:
qas.a:
A. An approved gas mask
B. An emergency kit for controlling leaks in fittings on the dome cover plate
C. Both A and B
D. Either A or B, but not both

qas.q: Carriers are required to give each driver who transports Division 1.1 or Division 1.2:
qas.a:
A. An extra fire bottle
B. The consignee's phone number
C. A copy of FMCSR, Part 397
D. A list of rest stops the driver may use

qas.q: When you're handling packages of explosives, you must:
qas.a: Never use hooks or other metal tools

qas.q: If the word "Forbidden" appears in the hazard class column of an entry in Hazardous Materials table:
qas.a: You must neve transport the product

qas.q: What is the largest allowable total transport index of all packages in a single vehicle allowed to be?
You're hauling 2000 pounds of a corrosive. You may park within ____ feet of the road if your work requires it and it's for a very short period of time
If you're hauling hazardous materials and you discover one of your tires is leaking air, you must:
qas.a: Stop at the nearest safe place and fix it

qas.q: You should stop before crossing a railroad grade if your vehicle is carrying how many gallons of chlorine?
qas.a: Any amount

qas.q: Animal and human foodstuffs must not be loaded in the same vehicle with:
qas.a: Poisons

qas.q: When fueling a placarded vehicle, someone must always be:
qas.a: At the nozzle, controlling the fuel flow

qas.q: Who do the regulations say is responsible for checking that the shipper correctly named, labeled and marked a hazardous material shipment?
qas.a: The carrier

qas.q: While transporting hazardous materials you stop to eat lunch at a truck stop. When you're in the truck stop, the shipping papers must be placed on the driver's seat or:
qas.a: In the driver's door pouch

qas.q: During an en-route inspection, you discover an overheated tire. If you're hauling hazardous materials, you must:
qas.a: Remove the tire and place it a safe distance from the vehicle

qas.q: When hauling hazardous materials, you must stop your vehicle and check any dual tires at least once every:
qas.a: 2 hours or 80 miles

qas.q: During a hazardous materials emergency, you must use every available means to:
qas.a:
A. Keep people away
B. Prevent smoking and keep open flame away
C. Warn others of the danger
D. All of the above

qas.q: If you're transporting division 1.1 or 1.2 explosives, you must not:
qas.a: Transport them in vehicle combinations if there is a placarded cargo tank in the combinationEn
